database_info.name: DSPPA IP Network PA Selection Theory

1_ip_pa_vs_traditional.principle: IP PA distributes audio over existing LAN infrastructure vs dedicated speaker cable

comparison.infrastructure: Dedicated speaker cable from amplifier to each speaker

comparison.best_for: Multi-building campus, large distributed systems, remote management
comparison.cost: Higher per endpoint but lower infrastructure for large deployments

MAG_series.features: Full-featured: scheduling, paging zones, emergency, music

POE_series.best_for: Retrofit, adding network audio to existing PoE infrastructure

3_mag_system_architecture.topology: Star: All terminals connect to LAN switch → MAG808 server manages all zones via IP

5_zone_design.principle: Each MAG terminal is an independent network zone
5_zone_design.advantage: Unlimited zones without additional amplifier hardware
5_zone_design.example: University campus: 50 buildings × 3 zones each = 150 MAG terminals, all managed from single MAG808 server

6_emergency_integration.principle: IP PA can integrate with fire alarm for emergency paging
6_emergency_integration.method: Fire panel dry contact → MAG808 server → triggers emergency message to all/selected zones
6_emergency_integration.note: For EN54 compliance, dedicated PAVA system (PAVA8500/9500) is required alongside or instead of IP PA

7_scheduling.principle: MAG808 supports automated scheduling of announcements and music

8_paging_stations.principle: Network paging stations allow remote microphone access from any location

models.MAG2107C: Desktop paging station with zone selection and microphone
models.RM20: Rack-mount paging microphone (analog — connects to local amplifier)

9_integration_with_traditional_pa.principle: IP PA can coexist with traditional 100V PA systems
9_integration_with_traditional_pa.method: MAG6812/6825/6835/6865 terminals have 100V/70V amplifier output → connect to existing passive speaker infrastructure. This allows gradual migration from traditional to IP PA.

standards.scope: PoE power standard for network speakers
